title: Warm Lead Caller / Setter – Fast Response Role | US Hours + Weekends | Base + Commission
description:
In this role, speed matters.



When a lead comes in, timing d
 everything — and your job is to make sure no opportunity is missed.



At Acquisition Ascension, we’re hiring a Warm Lead Caller / Appointment Setter to handle inbound, interested leads. These are people who have already taken action — your role is to respond quickly, qualify them, and make sure they get to the call.



This isn’t about volume.

It’s about responsibility, follow-through, and consistency.



What You’ll Be Doing:

Call new leads immediately after they come in

Qualify and book leads who haven’t scheduled yet

Confirm booked calls and ensure they’re prepared

Call leads again ~1 hour before their appointment to lock in attendance

Keep CRM notes clean and updated

Stay available during US time zones + weekends



What You’ll Get:

Base pay + commission structure

Warm, high-intent leads — no cold outreach

Clear scripts, workflows, and expectations

A role where consistency directly impacts results



You’re a Strong Fit If You:

Speak fluent English and sound confident on calls

Have experience in appointment setting, inbound calling, or sales

Are fast to respond — you don’t let leads sit

Are comfortable working US hours and weekends

Stay organized and follow through on every lead

Understand that missed follow-ups = missed revenue



How to Apply:

Send a short Loom (1–2 minutes) answering:



Your experience with inbound or warm lead calling

Your availability for US hours + weekend coverage

How quickly you typically act on new leads



We’re hiring one setter for this role.

If you’re someone who moves fast, stays consistent, and takes ownership — apply now.



— Pranav

Acquisition Ascension

title: Senior SEO Content Writer - Must DO Application Requirements
description:
Role Overview



We are hiring a Senior SEO Content Writer to take full ownership of content that drives organic traffic, rankings, and conversions.



This is not a generic writing role. You will be responsible for understanding search intent, building content structure, and producing content that performs.



You must be able to think strategically, execute independently, and consistently produce content aligned with both SEO and brand voice.



A strong understanding of EEAT (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) is required.



Key Responsibilities

Plan and execute SEO content strategy based on business goals

Conduct keyword research, SERP analysis, and search intent mapping

Build detailed content outlines and briefs before writing

Write high-quality, long-form content (blogs, landing pages, guides)

Edit and refine content to improve clarity, structure, and performance

Optimize existing content for rankings, CTR, and conversions

Implement strong on-page SEO (headings, internal linking, structure)

Apply EEAT principles through depth, accuracy, and credible sourcing

Use AI tools effectively by creating, refining, and improving prompts

Track content performance and iterate based on data

Writing, Editing, and Brand Voice

Must be able to quickly understand and adapt to different brand voices

Strong ability to maintain tone, consistency, and positioning across content

Excellent editing skills — able to improve clarity, flow, and readability

High attention to detail in grammar, sentence structure, and formatting

Mastery of prepositions, punctuation, and written English is required

Able to turn rough inputs into clear, structured, on-brand content

Industry Exposure



You should be comfortable writing across multiple industries. Please show experience in areas such as:



SaaS

Agencies / marketing services

Local businesses

E-commerce

B2B services

Any technical or specialized industries



You must be able to research and produce authoritative content in unfamiliar domains.



Requirements

4–6+ years of experience in SEO content writing

Proven track record of ranking content on Google

Strong understanding of search intent, on-page SEO, and content structure

Clear understanding and application of EEAT in content

Strong ability to create, modify, and refine AI prompts for content generation

Excellent command of English, including grammar, punctuation, and sentence construction

Experience with tools such as Ahrefs, SEMrush, Surfer, Clearscope, or similar

Strong research and analytical thinking

KPIs

Organic traffic growth

Keyword rankings (Top 3 / Top 10)

Content-driven conversions

Quality and consistency of output

Performance improvements on updated content

Application Requirements



To be considered, you must include the following:



3–5 SEO articles you have written that are currently ranking. Include target keywords and performance details if available

A short breakdown of one article:

Search intent

Why it performed (or didn’t)

What you would improve



A structured content outline for:

"SEO agency for small business" (Warren Digital)



Your outline must include:



Primary and secondary search intent

Target audience and stage of awareness

Logical heading structure (H1–H3)

Key sections required to compete with top-ranking pages

How you would differentiate the content

Where and how you would include conversion elements (CTAs, trust signals)

EEAT signals you would incorporate (case studies, proof, authority, etc.)

A Loom video (5–10 minutes) where you:

Analyze a top-ranking article for a keyword

Explain why it ranks

Break down structure, intent, and SEO elements

Identify EEAT signals

Explain how you would outperform it

List the industries you have written for

title: RECRUITER (NON-IT) – REMOTE
description:
Overview



This position requires a recruiter who is comfortable conducting proactive outreach and cold contact with candidates via phone,
, and LinkedIn.



We are a boutique U.S.-based recruiting firm seeking an experienced Recruiter with a strong background particularly in professional and management roles (non-IT recruiting).



This role primarily supports our Construction Worldwide division, which focuses on recruiting construction management professionals and executive-level candidates. From time to time, recruiting may also include general corporate roles (G&A) such as accounting, HR, Sales or administrative professionals.



Remote full-time role with long-term potential for the right person, based on performance.



This role is ideal for a recruiter who is resourceful, proactive, and comfortable actively reaching out to candidates through multiple channels, including phone,
, and LinkedIn.

________________________________________



WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING



Candidate Sourcing

Identify and engage qualified candidates for roles including:

• Construction Management

   (Project Managers, Project Engineers, Superintendents, 

   Estimators, Architects — no labor roles)

• Executive & Management positions

• Accounting & Finance

• Office & Administrative roles

• Human Resources

• Sales & Marketing

________________________________________



Candidate Research



• Identify and verify candidate contact information, including personal and work details

• Use online research and sourcing tools to locate potential candidates

• Maintain organized candidate records in our ATS/CRM system

• Document candidate interactions and recruiting activity

________________________________________



Candidate Outreach



• Conduct proactive outreach via: Phone, LinkedIn,

• Screen candidates to evaluate qualifications and interest

• Maintain clear notes and status updates within the ATS

Important:

This role requires a recruiter who is comfortable proactively contacting candidates both at their personal and professional contact points when appropriate.

________________________________________



Candidate Screening & Process Management



• Conduct initial candidate screening conversations

• Evaluate experience and overall fit for client roles

• Coordinate next steps in the recruiting process

• Assist with reference checks and candidate follow-up when needed

________________________________________



Future Scope



• Research potential companies and hiring managers

• Identify job opportunities for strong candidates

• Support recruiting efforts across additional divisions as needed

________________________________________



REQUIREMENTS



Experience

• Minimum of 5+ years of recruiting experience (agency recruiting preferred)

• Experience recruiting professional or management-level roles

• Experience using ATS/CRM systems (PCRecruiter preferred)

________________________________________



Skills



• Strong sourcing and candidate research abilities

• Advanced Boolean search skills

• Excellent spoken and written English 

• Strong phone presence and communication skills

• Ability to build rapport quickly with candidates

• Excellent organizational and documentation habits

• Ability to follow instructions carefully and consistently

• Self-motivated and able to work independently after training

________________________________________



Technical Skills



• LinkedIn and online sourcing platforms

• Google Docs

• MS Word / Excel

• MS Teams / Zoom or similar tools

• ATS / CRM systems

• Experience using contact finder tools



Preferred but not required:

• LinkedIn Recruiter access

________________________________________



Additional Requirements



• Excellent spoken and written English

• Typing speed 50+ WPM preferred

• Strong internet research skills

• Reliable high-speed internet connection

• Personal computer suitable for remote work



Highly valued:

• Experience recruiting construction management professionals

________________________________________



SCHEDULE



• Remote position

• Work hours aligned with U.S. Pacific Time (PST/PDT)

• Full-time

• 8+ hours per day

• Availability between 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM PT

________________________________________

COMPENSATION



• Hourly pay: $3.50 – $7.00 per hour

  (approximately $600 – $1,200 per month, depending on hours and experience)



• Applicants should indicate their desired hourly rate

• Bonus opportunities may be available based on base compensation and performance

________________________________________



HOW TO APPLY



Please submit the following:

1. Resume including your LinkedIn profile

2. Roles you have recruited for in the past

        (industries, titles, locations, compensation ranges)

3. Sourcing methods and platforms you typically use

4. ATS/CRM systems you have worked with

5. Any sourcing tools you subscribe to

6. Your desired hourly rate (minimum and ideal)

7. Your availability (U.S. Pacific Time hours)

8. A short voice recording introducing yourself and sharing one recruiting success story

________________________________________

title: Customer Experience & Operations Associate (E-commerce Coffee Company) *Full Time, Remote | Long-Term Growth Opportunity
description:
Are you ready…

To start a role that motivates and excites you?

And a position where we invest in your learning, education, and growth?



Then keep reading, we want to work with you!





WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS POSITION:

This is not your typical support role — you’ll work directly with our founders, influence company-wide systems, and gain exposure to every part of the business.



Incredible company culture:

• Small, close team with collaborative, friendly and open culture.

• We believe the biggest asset of a company is our people. We focus on helping each tea
mber reach their full potential by investing in their learning, growth, skills, and education. When we grow as individuals, we succeed as a team.

• Not a fan of office politics or drama? We’re on the same page. Instead of wasting time with that, we focus on solving problems and reaching goals

• 100% remote, enjoy the comfort and flexibility of working from home



Pay, benefits, progression, and long-term growth:

• Competitive salary

• Benefits including up to 16 days a year Paid Time off

• Progression as we grow: greater responsibilities, leadership opportunities, and increased pay.

• Feel empowered, make decisions, have an impact

• Grow with the company and live up to your potential!

• Join an exciting, fast-growing company!







ABOUT US:

We’re a young, ambitious, and small (but growing!) company that’s making a massive impact in ecommerce. 



Since 2015 we’ve been obsessed with building award-winning products and awesome brands that people fall in love with. We have 2 primary brands in our portfolio: 

(1) an international award-winning coffee products brand 
 / 
).

(2) an educational coaching program that helps entrepreneurs grow their businesses to create a positive impact in the world!



Our two recent flagship products became the world’s most successful launches in their categories. We’ve shipped more than 200,000 orders worldwide and we’re continuing to expand.



Our culture is transparent and candid. You’re always welcome (and encouraged!) to ask questions or express a difference of opinion. Learning and growth is a cornerstone of our culture, both personally and professionally. We work hard, and we have fun!







WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

We’re looking for a proactive and detail-oriented team player to fill this role—someone who thrives on solving problems, staying organized, and making things run smoothly across departments.



Do you love helping customers, supporting a team, and keeping operations organized? Do you get satisfaction from making systems work better and “getting things done”? If yes, we want you on our team!



You’re probably the type of person who:

• Enjoys supporting others and finding solutions to problems

• Has strong communication and organizational skills

• Likes working behind the scenes to make sure everything runs like clockwork

• Is excited to wear multiple hats and be part of a growing, fast-moving company

• Has worked directly with founders or in a startup, and enjoys that fast-paced, flexible vibe

We’re not just looking for someone who can support our team, we’re looking for someone to be proactive with taking ownership of tasks, solve problems independently, and grow into larger responsibilities over time.







YOUR OPPORTUNITY AND ROLE ON THE TEAM:

Our culture is collaborative, friendly, and open. We hire people who are smart, ambitious, and have a strong desire to learn and grow.



Sound like you? This is an exciting long-term opportunity!



This is a fully remote position. You’ll work directly with our founders and collaborate with our small but mighty team to support customer happiness, streamline operations, and take on projects that help the business grow. 



This is a foundational role — one where you’ll gain visibility into all parts of the company.



As you grow, your role will grow with you, based on your interests and strengths:

• Want to work improving our systems and processes? Awesome!

• Have ideas on how to improve our website? Fantastic!

• Want to learn how to manage projects? Let’s go!



We’re not just hiring for a role — we’re hiring for potential. 



Your responsibilities will span 4 key areas: customer experience (30-40%), operations/logistics (20-30%), executive support (10-20%), and special projects (10-20%) — giving you exposure across the business. These percentages are estimates and you can expect your day-to-day to shift and evolve as the business grows and as you take ownership. 



1. Customer Experience

• Provide
/chat-based support for our customers across our channels (Shopify, Amazon, and wholesale)

• Answer order and product questions, resolve issues promptly, and ensure customers feel heard and supported

• Support wholesale partners with order updates, invoicing, and coordination



2. Order Management & Platform Coordination

• Manage, monitor, and troubleshoot orders across Shopify, Amazon, and wholesale channels

• Troubleshoot and resolve shipping or fulfillment issues with partners and customers

• Maintain accurate product listings, inventory data, and logistics information across platforms



3. Executive & Admin Support

• Manage founder and team inboxes, schedule meetings, and organize internal documentation

• Assist with travel bookings, meeting preparation, and note-taking

• Support small projects by tracking timelines, managing tasks, and ensuring follow-through



4. Special Projects & Company Operations

• Conduct research projects to support decision-making (tools, systems, competitors)

• Support lead generation efforts through identifying new wholesale prospects, brand partners, or influencers

• Support planning and execution of events, expos, or marketing campaigns — manage timelines, logistics, and tasks

• Document and maintain SOPs for operational clarity and training

• Test and troubleshoot web pages, workflows, or customer journeys to ensure quality and functionality

• Take ownership of ad hoc “figure it out” tasks — we’ll guide you, but we value initiative and independent problem-solving mindset



You’ll be trained on all platforms and tools, and we’ll support your learning — but we’re looking for someone who’s excited to take initiative and build systems along the way. This role evolves — as you grow, so will your responsibilities.







ABOUT YOU / REQUIRED SKILLS OR EXPERIENCE:

We value curiosity, reliability, and the ability to take ownership. If you enjoy figuring things out, learning on the job, and taking initiative, you’ll thrive here. This role is not a fit if you need constant direction or struggle to manage your own time.



Must have experience:

• 2+ years of experience in a customer service or operations support role 
/chat-based)

• 1+ years experience in eCommerce environments (Shopify, Amazon, etc.)

• Fluent in written and spoken English



Key traits:

• Strong detail orientation, organizational, and time-management skills — you’re detail-obsessed and on top of things

• Proactive problem-solver — you take initiative, do the research, and follow through

• Able to work independently, ask questions when needed, and communicate clearly

• Comfortable with constructive feedback and direct, respectful communication



Optional but a big plus:

• Experience coordinating projects, events, or marketing campaigns is a plus

• Experience with executive assistant work 
/calendar/travel management)

• Experience improving or documenting processes (SOPs)

• Experience at a startup or small business where you wore multiple hats

• “Experience coordinating projects, events, or marketing campaigns is a plus”







PAY, WORKING HOURS, HOW TO APPLY

If this description seems like it’s too intimidating for you, this position is probably not for you. However, if you’re still here reading and this sounds exciting for you, we’d love to meet you!



The full time monthly salary will be $
based on your skills and level of relevant experience. Being a member of our team has other benefits including up to 16 days of paid time off per year.



We understand you may have a busy schedule, so we’ve made our interview process extremely quick and efficient. 



To apply, please follow the steps below:

1. Get started with a quick Initial Screening here for us to get to know you: 

2. Complete the screening and we’ll be in touch with next steps once we receive it.





The position is on a first come first review basis, so the sooner you complete the screening, the more likely you'll get hired.



Good luck, and we hope to have someone awesome like you to join us!

title: Social Media & Community Lead (Junior-Level, Multi-Function)
description:
About Us

We are a woman-owned creative studio based in Los Angeles, California working with VTubers, streamers, content creators, and digital brands.

Our focus is building internet-native media such as motion graphics, intro videos, creator branding, thumbnails, announcement videos, website visuals, and social media content.



We are a small but growing creative studio that values collaboration, creativity, and initiative. We are looking for individuals who understand online culture and want to grow with a team from the ground up.



This role will start as contract work while we build a workflow together, with the potential for long-term collaboration as the studio grows.



The Role

You will work under and report directly to the Creative Director while collaborating with the Creative Hub team.



This is a part-time, gig-based role focused on supporting social media growth, content research, and community development for both our studio and partnered VTubers.



You will help represent VTubers who are looking for support and growth, assisting in building their online presence and communities. This role also involves contributing ideas, identifying trends, analyzing performance, and helping shape content direction over time.



A strong understanding of social media platforms, analytics, VTuber/streamer culture, and 
 communities is required.



What You Will Be Working On



Projects and tasks may include:



• Managing and posting content for the studio and partnered VTubers

• Repurposing clips into short-form content (TikTok,
/X, Instagram, YouTube Shorts)

• Researching trends in VTuber, streaming, and creator communities

• Analyzing post performance (views, engagement, retention) to improve content

• Assisting with content planning and improvements

• Helping build and organize 
 communities

• Supporting engagement and interaction across platforms

• Assisting with event ideas, announcements, and community activities



Responsibilities

• Post and schedule content across multiple platforms (X, TikTok, Instagram, YouTube,
, etc.)

• Coordinate with the video editing team to identify, refine, and repurpose clips into high-performing short-form content

• Assist in identifying trends and contribute to content planning, formats, and posting strategies

• Help forecast content ideas and community initiatives based on trends and audience behavior

• Provide input on content and community decisions based on research and performance insights

• Track and review analytics (views, engagement, growth) to suggest improvements



• Help represent and support VTubers through social media and community efforts

• Support 
 setup, organization, and moderation

• Monitor tone, language, and engagement within communities



• Oversee and support 
 moderators and junior social media/admin staff

• Establish and maintain standards, guidelines, and community tone for different VTuber and brand communities

• Ensure a consistent and on-brand “vibe” across all platforms and communities



• Assist with planning events, announcements, and community activities

• Coordinate with the Creative Hub team on:

-- streamer schedules

-- content releases

-- advertisements and promotional posts



• Work collaboratively using tools such as Trello and 

• Report directly to the Creative Director and follow assigned direction



What We Are Looking For

• Strong understanding of social media platforms (X/Twitter, TikTok, Instagram, YouTube,
)

• Familiarity with 
 and online community management

• Understanding of VTuber, streaming, and internet culture

• Basic understanding of analytics (views, engagement, trends, performance)

• Ability to follow direction while contributing ideas

• Reliable communication in a remote work environment



Formal experience is not required, but strong interest and understanding are important.



Preferred

• Experience managing or posting on social media accounts

• Experience participating in or moderating 
 communities

• Understanding of trends, 
s, and online engagement

• Interest in VTubers, anime-style content, or streaming culture

• Familiarity with platforms like Fiverr or creator marketplaces

• Basic coding or technical skills (e.g., 
 bots, simple scripts, automation tools) that can support workflow or community features



Work Setup

• Remote work

• Contract position

• Part-time / gig-based (up to 20 hours per week depending on workload)

• Flexible schedule



This role has the potential to transition into a longer-term position based on performance and team fit.



Compensation



Junior Social Media & Community Lead (Entry Level)

• $1.20 – $4.00 USD per hour depending on experience

• Up to 20 hours per week depending on workload



The first 30 days will serve as a trial period to establish workflow and expectations.

Pay increases may be considered later based on performance and long-term collaboration.



How to Apply



Please include:



• A short introduction about yourself

• Any relevant experience (social media, 
, content, etc.)

• Examples of accounts you have managed or contributed to (if available)

Which VTuber, streamer, or content creator do you think has strong social media and community presence, and why?



Important

Applications that use AI-generated answers or fail to answer the question will be ignored.

title: Social Media Specialist / Influencer Marketing
description:
Position Overview

We are seeking an Influencer Marketing & Social Media Specialist to expand our online presence and strengthen brand awareness through influencer partnerships and social media management. This role involves sourcing and managing influencers, coordinating content, and tracking performance, while also executing day-to-day social media tasks across
, TikTok, X, Instagram, and YouTube. The ideal candidate is creative, organized, and passionate about digital trends, pop culture, and community engagement.



Key Responsibilities:

A. Influencer Marketing

-Source, vet, and onboard influencers within relevant communities (trading cards, Funko Pop, collectibles, gaming).

-Negotiate collaboration terms and manage influencer partnerships across TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube.

-Provide influencers with briefs and creative guidance to ensure brand alignment.

-Coordinate deliverables and ensure timely publishing of influencer content.

-Track and analyze influencer performance, engagement, and ROI.

-Optimize influencer strategies based on campaign results and industry trends.

-Maintain detailed influencer records, contracts, and communications.

B.Social Media Management

-Execute social media-related tasks across
, TikTok, X, Instagram, and YouTube.

-Schedule and publish posts across platforms in line with marketing campaigns.

-Respond to social media inbox messages and comments in a timely, professional manner.

-Engage with followers and online communities to foster interaction and brand loyalty.

-Support the Marketing Team in executing community engagement and content initiatives.



Qualifications:

-Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Digital Media, or related field (preferred).

-1–3 years of experience in influencer marketing, social media management, or brand partnerships.

, TikTok, X, Instagram, and YouTube.

Skills & Competencies:

title: Virtual Assistant (Sales & Marketing Operations)
description:

We’re building from the ground up, and this role is for someone who wants to grow with a brand early, not just “do tasks.”

If you like ownership, structure, and figuring things out—you’ll thrive here.

s



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE:

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

Required

WORK DETAILS:

* Monday–Friday

WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT:

* High ownership and visibility

* Clear path to growth and higher compensation



HOW TO APPLY:

Please fill out the skills assessment survey/ application form:

title: Marketing Assistant — Digital Marketing Agency (Remote, Full-Time)
description:
This is an entry-level marketing role built for someone who wants to learn digital marketing by actually doing it — inside a real agency, on real client accounts, with a team that will train you properly and invest in your growth.



We're not looking for someone who already knows everything. We're looking for someone who follows instructions precisely, executes with care, and takes pride in getting the details right every single time.



If you're a recent graduate or early-career professional who is coachable, highly organized, and ready to commit fully to a long-term role — read on.



What This Role Is:



You'll work inside a structured marketing system, executing weekly deliverables across website management, local SEO,
/SMS marketing, and client reporting. Much of your work involves recurring tasks with documented processes — and following those processes precisely is a real skill we take seriously.



But we're not looking for someone who needs to be told what to do next. The best people on our team see what needs to be done, do it, and flag anything that doesn't look right. They take ownership of their work, not just their task list.



Success in this role looks like: consistent execution, zero dropped balls, and a quiet confidence that if something falls under your responsibility — it's handled.



What You'll Do:



In the first month, you'll shadow our Marketing Manager and learn our systems hands-on. By month two, you'll be executing independently. By month three, you'll own a full client workload.



Day-to-day responsibilities include:



- Update client websites in WordPress (specials, events, gallery, blog posts, landing pages)

- Manage Google Business Profiles (photo uploads, review replies)

- Collect and organize weekly reporting data (metric screenshots, tracking sheet updates)

- Support
 and SMS campaign execution in our retention platform (full training provided)

- Maintain content calendars and ensure deliverables stay on schedule

- Log tasks and progress in ClickUp daily



What We're Looking For:



Non-negotiable:



- Conscientious and detail-oriented — you catch your own errors, you don't cut corners, and you care about quality in routine work

- Ability to follow SOPs — you can read a documented process and execute it the same way every time

- Strong written English — clear, professional communication; able to produce client-facing content without heavy editing

- AI proficiency — comfortable using ChatGPT, Claude, or similar tools as part of your daily workflow

- Reliable internet and computer

- Fully available — this role requires your complete professional focus from month two onward (40 hours/week, Monday–Friday)



Helpful but trainable:



- Experience with WordPress, Google Sheets, or
 marketing platforms

- Canva or basic design skills

- Familiarity with local SEO concepts

- Google Business Profile management experience

- A college degree in marketing, communications, business, or a related field is preferred — not because credentials matter more than character, but because this role attracts people who want to build a real career in marketing, and we want someone who sees this as a starting point, not a stopgap.



A Note on Commitment:



We'll be direct: this is a full-time position that requires your complete, undivided professional focus — especially from month two onward at 40 hours per week. We're a small, high-output team and we need someone who is fully in.



We've hired people in the past who treated this like a side job. That doesn't work here. If you have existing obligations that would prevent you from committing fully, please be upfront about that before we begin the process.



For the right person — someone who takes ownership, communicates proactively, and shows up fully — this is a long-term role with real room to grow in both responsibilities and compensation. We promote from within. The last person hired into this role was promoted to Marketing Manager within one year.



If you're an A-player ready to commit, this is a place where your work will actually matter.



Compensation & Hours:



Month 1 (Training Period):

- 30 hours/week

- $600/month

- Shadow our Marketing Manager, learn all systems, begin supervised execution



Month 2 onward:

- 40 hours/week

- $800/month



Month 6:

- Performance review with opportunity for a raise



Hours are flexible within the Monday–Friday window. We care about output and on-time delivery, not when you're online. Time and PTO are tracked via a time-tracking app.



Benefits:

- 13th month pay

- 10 paid holidays per year

- Paid Time Off (begins accruing after probation period)

- Fully remote

- Flexible schedule

- Growth path — we promote from within and reward people who invest in the role



About LeafSuite:



LeafSuite (
) is a US-based digital marketing agency that serves independent cannabis dispensaries. We help dispensary owners grow their customer base and revenue through retention marketing, local SEO, and website operations.

Cannabis is a legal, regulated industry in the United States, and all of our clients are licensed businesses. This role requires genuine openness to learning about cannabis culture, products, and the dispensary industry — not just tolerating it, but engaging with it well enough to do good work for our clients. If that's not something you're comfortable with, this role isn't the right fit.



How to Apply:



Apply with the subject line: Focused on Marketing – [Your Name]



Include:

- A brief overview of your marketing background or education

- An example of a recurring task you've owned and how you made sure it got done consistently

- Confirmation that this would be your sole professional commitment

- Your ideal working hours (when you get your best work done).



Please write your application in your own words. We're not looking for a polished, perfect response — we're looking for yours. An application that sounds like it was written by an AI tells us nothing about you. Imperfect sentences that are genuinely yours tell us a lot. If we can't hear your voice in your application, we'll move on.



Bonus points if you include your DISC assessment results — either displayed on your profile or attached to your application. Not required, but it helps us get to know you faster.



We review every application. We respond to candidates who follow the instructions.



Thank you — and have a great day!

title: Virtual Assistant (Canva Specialist for Digital Products) – Remote
description:

title: DM Lead Qualifier & Appointment Setter for Premium Coaching Service (Instagram + Facebook Inbound Leads)
description:
Overview:



We are looking for a highly organized and fast-moving DM lead qualifier and appointment setter to manage inbound leads coming from physical therapy content on Instagram and
.



Your role is to take incoming attention and turn it into qualified booked sales calls.



This is NOT cold outreach. All leads come inbound from content.



?



Responsibilities:



1. Lead Monitoring

 • Monitor Instagram and 
ments and DMs daily

 • Identify users engaging with content and initiating conversations

 • Ensure no inbound lead is missed or left unanswered



?



2. DM Engagement & Filtering

 • Respond to inbound DMs using structured messaging

 • Ask simple qualification questions based on a provided system

 • Quickly determine whether a lead is serious or not serious

 • Filter out all low-quality, curious, or time-wasting inquiries



?



3. Qualification

 • Identify high-intent individuals based on:

 • severity of issue

 • urgency

 • commitment level

 • seriousness of solving the problem



?



4. Price Framing (Mandatory Step)

 • Clearly set expectations about the nature of the service

 • Communicate that this is a structured, high-level 90-day system

 • Frame the investment level early so only serious buyers continue



?



5. Appointment Booking

 • Book all qualified leads directly onto a calendar

 • Ensure leads are confirmed and ready before scheduling

 • Maintain clean handoff to sales calls



?



Core Objective:



You are responsible for ensuring that only high-quality, serious, and qualified leads reach the sales call stage.



Your job is to remove:

 • tire kickers

 • curiosity inquiries

 • low-intent conversations

 • time-wasters



And pass forward:

 • serious buyers

 • motivated individuals

 • people ready to take action



?



Key Expectations:

 • Fast response time to inbound messages

 • Strict adherence to messaging and filtering process

 • No long conversations or unnecessary explanations

 • No coaching or advice inside DMs

 • No negotiating or changing the offer structure

 • Maintain control of conversations at all times



?



Ideal Candidate:

 • Strong written communication skills

 • Experience handling inbound leads or appointment setting

 • Comfortable working in fast-paced DM environments

 • Highly disciplined and structured communicator

 • Able to follow a system exactly without improvisation



?



Important Notes:



This role is a critical part of a high-performance sales system. Your job is to ensure that only the most qualified leads are passed through for closing.



You are the first layer of the sales process and control the quality of opportunities entering the pipeline.



Please answer these questions:



Have you worked as a DM setter or appointment setter for a high-ticket offer before?

 If yes, what niche was it (fitness, coaching, real estate, etc.) and what was the typical price range of the offer?

In your previous role, were you responsible for qualifying leads before booking calls, or were you mainly focused on booking as many calls as possible?

Can you walk me through what you think makes a high-quality lead in a DM setting environment? What signals tell you someone is worth booking vs not?

If someone is interested but clearly not willing or able to invest in a high-ticket program, what would you do in the conversation with them?

How would you handle someone who keeps asking questions in DMs but avoids committing to a call?

How do you naturally bring up or identify whether someone is financially qualified without sounding pushy?

In your past role, what metrics were you tracked on? (booked calls, show-up rate, closing rate, etc.) What did you personally focus on improving?

Are you comfortable telling someone they are not a good fit for a program? If yes, how would you communicate that in a professional way?

How quickly can you respond to inbound DMs during working hours, and how do you stay consistent with high message volume?

Let’s say a 45-year-old man messages saying he wants to fix his shoulder, but he keeps saying he ‘needs to think about investing money’ and avoids giving a clear yes or no. Walk me through exactly what you would say step-by-step.

Why do you think you would be a good fit for a high-ticket coaching business where your main job is to filter leads and only bring in serious buyers?

title: Paid Media Specialist
description:
About the Role



As a Paid Media Specialist (Meta Ads), you will act as the execution engine behind our paid social channel across multiple high-volume, design-led brands.



This is a hands-on, platform-focused role where you will be responsible for building, launching, and maintaining Meta ad campaigns with a high level of precision and consistency. You will work closely with the Senior Marketing Manager and Head of Performance Marketing, translating fully briefed creative assets into clean, structured, and correctly configured campaigns.



This role is best suited for someone who thrives in execution-focused work, values accuracy, and is confident operating inside Meta Ads Manager on a daily basis.





Key Responsibilities

Campaign Building



Build campaigns, ad sets, and ads using fully briefed creative assets

Configure all campaign elements accurately, including objectives, audiences, placements, budgets, schedules, and tracking

Use AI tools to generate on-brand ad copy (headlines, descriptions, and CTAs)

Apply strict naming conventions consistently across all accounts and funnel stages (TOFU, MOFU, BOFU)

Publish campaigns and confirm successful launch with the Senior Marketing Manager

Identify and flag any asset specification issues, disapprovals, or delivery concerns immediately

Ensure all builds are accurate and require no rework

Budget Adjustments & Campaign Maintenance



Execute budget changes, reallocations, and optimizations as directed by the Head of Performance Marketing

Pause, activate, and duplicate campaigns, ad sets, or ads as instructed

Implement adjustments to placements, schedules, or audiences when required

Monitor campaign delivery and flag any irregularities such as spend drops, disapprovals, or account-level alerts

Platform Hygiene & Quality Assurance



Perform daily account health checks across all ad accounts

Maintain clean and structured campaign architecture (no duplication, misconfigurations, or orphaned assets)

Ensure pixel tracking and UTM parameters are correctly implemented on all campaigns

Enforce strict naming conventions across all builds

Provide regular updates or summaries to the Senior Marketing Manager via Slack 



Other Responsibilities



Complete additional tasks and platform-related requests as assigned

Maintain accuracy and attention to detail across all campaign builds and updates

Communicate proactively when issues, blockers, or uncertainties arise

Contribute to maintaining a structured, efficient, and high-performing ad account environment





About You



Minimum of 3 years’ hands-on experience using Meta Ads Manager on a daily basis

Strong experience managing multiple ad accounts or brands simultaneously

Deep understanding of campaign setup, including objectives, audiences, placements, and budget optimisation

Highly detail-oriented with a strong focus on accuracy, naming conventions, and tracking setup

Able to execute quickly while maintaining a high standard of work

Comfortable working as an execution-focused tea
mber, following clear direction

Strong communication skills with the ability to raise issues and ask clarifying questions when needed

Experience in e-commerce or home, renovation, or lifestyle brands is an advantage





Finer Details

Schedule: Part-time (1 day per week / 8 hours)

Start Date: TBD

title: Lead STR Operations VA
description:
Lead STR Operations VA



Senior-level only — not a beginner role



$6–$8/hr USD

Senior / Lead level

Remote — Philippines

3–4 days/week · 12-hr shifts

Biweekly pay · Long-term

ABOUT THIS ROLE



We manage 11 active short-term rental properties across Tennessee and Florida, with a 12th coming in Kentucky. We are hiring a senior-level STR Operations VA to become the lead operational support person across the entire portfolio.



This is not a basic guest messaging role, and it is not a role for someone who simply follows scripts. We need someone who is highly organized, calm under pressure, strong at problem solving, able to build and improve systems, detail-oriented enough to catch mistakes before owners or guests do, and flexible enough to work within owner preferences and property-specific exceptions.



This person will be my right-hand operator. Over time, the right person should be capable of helping train and onboard another VA, with operations eventually flowing through them.



WHO THIS ROLE IS FOR



You may be a fit if you:



Have 3+ years of hands-on Airbnb / STR operations experience

Have worked in a lead VA, senior support, QA, trainer, or operations role

Have directly managed cleaners, maintenance, vendors, and guest escalations

Can identify what needs to be verified before acting — not after

Can solve problems quickly and communicate clearly under pressure

Are comfortable calling vendors, cleaners, or guests when needed

Want a long-term role with growth and leadership opportunity

Can recommend improvements without forcing your own style on a client

THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR

Beginners or entry-level VAs

Script-only Airbnb agents who do not understand context

People who need constant step-by-step direction

People who are reactive but not organized

People who think there is only one right way to run STRs

People who override owner preferences instead of learning how the client wants things done



We are looking for someone who can bring structure, judgment, and leadership — without a my way or the highway mindset.



WHAT THIS ROLE INCLUDES

Guest messaging and issue resolution across Airbnb, VRBO, and direct bookings

Verifying door codes match guest reservations and Schlage is correctly configured for every check-in

Camera monitoring at check-in and during stays — verifying guest count and flagging violations

Managing cleaner communication, day-before reminders, and photo review after each turnover

Coordinating maintenance, vendor follow-up, and urgent issues

Daily use of OwnerRez, Hospitable, Turno, Schlage, Slack, and OpenPhone

Supporting operational dashboards, trackers, and follow-up systems

Creating, updating, and improving SOPs over time

Acting as senior operations partner — eventually helping train and onboard future VAs

PLATFORMS AND TOOLS

Airbnb

VRBO

OwnerRez

Hospitable

Turno

Schlage

Slack

OpenPhone

WhatsApp

PriceLabs

Safely

Baselane

SCHEDULE AND COMPENSATION



Wed-Sat 11-7:30p

Sunday 8a-4:30p

Pay is $6–$8/hr USD based on experience. This is a long-term role with growth potential.



IMPORTANT NOTE ABOUT THIS CLIENT



This client is detail-heavy and particular. Some properties have unique rules, quirks, and exceptions. We need someone who can build structure, stay organized, make thoughtful recommendations, respect client preferences, and avoid assuming every property should be run exactly the same way. Strong opinions are welcome. Rigidity is not.



HOW TO APPLY



Begin your application with this exact phrase: I understand this is a senior STR operations role. Applications that do not include this phrase will not be reviewed.



Please answer all of the following:



1. Describe your STR / Airbnb experience



Years of experience · number of properties · your exact role · platforms used



2. Describe a time you inherited a messy or disorganized STR setup



What was wrong · what you fixed first · how you improved it



3. Describe a time you worked with an owner whose preferences differed from how you would personally run things



How you handled it · how you balanced best practices with client expectations



4. Describe a guest issue that required coordinating with a cleaner, maintenance person, or vendor



What happened · what you did · what the outcome was



5. What kind of long-term role are you looking for, and what does growth mean to you?



VOICE SAMPLE REQUIRED



Because this role requires calling vendors and speaking with guests, a 60–90 second voice sample is required. Please record yourself answering: "Describe a guest issue you handled that required calling a cleaner or maintenance person. What was the problem, how did you handle the call, and what was the outcome?" Requirements: clear professional English, calm and confident tone, no background noise. Upload as an audio file or share a private link (Google Drive, Loom, or similar). Applications without a voice sample will not be considered.

title: Sales Associate / Sales Representative (Full-Time, Remote)
description:
Amane Social Connect

We’re looking for a confident, personable, and driven Sales Associate to join our growing team at Amane Social Connect—a digital marketing company helping businesses elevate their online presence and attract more clients through high-quality content and strategy.

This role is perfect for someone who enjoys speaking with business owners, building relationships, and confidently closing deals.

As our Sales Associate, you will take scheduled inbound calls with potential clients and guide them toward the best solution for their business—whether that’s our AI social media app or our full-service social media management.

You’ll be trained through short, easy-to-follow video modules and provided with a clear structure for calls, questions, and closing.

Take inbound sales calls (scheduled appointments) during 9 AM – 5 PM (NYC time)

Confidently present and sell:

Our social media app

Our full-service social media management packages

Build trust with clients and understand their business needs

Close deals and convert leads into paying clients

Complete light admin tasks after each call:

Sending intake forms

Organizing client information

Uploading raw content for the social media team

Ensure a smooth handoff to our content team after closing

???? Compensation

Base Pay: $150/month

Commission: $50/month per client you close (recurring for the duration of the client’s contract)

? The more clients you close and retain, the more your income grows consistently over time.

???? Schedule

Full-time: 40 hours per week

Calls scheduled between 9 AM – 5 PM EST (NYC time)

???? We’re Looking For Someone Who:

Has a friendly, confident, and positive personality

Is comfortable on camera and on calls

Can follow structure and training (no guesswork needed)

Is organized and able to complete simple admin tasks

Is motivated by performance and growth

???? Additional Details

This is a long-term opportunity with room to grow as the company scales

? About Us

At Amane Social Connect, we believe in helping businesses show up online like luxury brands—confident, polished, and impossible to ignore. We’re building a team that reflects that same energy.

title: Preferably FRESH GRADUATES – Petroleum / Chemical / Mechanical
description:
About the company

CoEN Solutions is a specialist energy consulting and technology company based in Singapore and Australia, delivering integrated subsurface, production, and digital solutions across the oil and gas sector. With expertise spanning reservoir engineering, production optimization, hydrocarbon accounting, and digital twin technologies, CoEN Solutions supports operators in maximizing asset value and improving operational efficiency. The company combines deep technical knowledge with advanced software and data-driven workflows to provide high-quality, practical solutions for complex energy projects globally.



We are looking for fresh graduates who are passionate about engineering and eager to build a career in the energy and oil & gas industry.

This is a great opportunity to join a technical team working on production optimisation, modelling, and digital transformation projects—even if you have no prior industry experience.



About the Role

As a Petroleum Engineer, you will be trained and mentored to work on real-world engineering projects, supporting the development of models and solutions that improve field performance and operational efficiency.



You’ll gain hands-on exposure to:



Production optimisation

Reservoir and production system modelling

Digital oilfield (smart field) technologies

Data-driven engineering workflows



What You Will Do

Assist in building and running engineering models and simulations

Support production optimisation and analysis tasks

Work with engineers to understand field data and system behavior

Learn and use industry tools (e.g. modelling and simulation software)

Contribute to technical reports and project deliverables

Collaborate with a team of experienced engineers and specialists



Who We’re Looking For



Fresh graduates only in the following field:



Petroleum Engineering

Chemical Engineering

Mechanical Engineering



Strong interest in oil & gas, energy, or engineering consulting

Good analytical and problem-solving skills

Willingness to learn new tools and technologies

Basic understanding of engineering fundamentals

Good communication and teamwork skills



Nice to Have (Not Required)

Internship or academic projects related to energy or modelling

Exposure to simulation tools or programming (Python, MATLAB, etc.)

Interest in data analytics, AI, or digital technologies



What We Offer

Structured training and mentorship program

Hands-on experience on real engineering projects

Exposure to advanced modelling and digital technologies

Opportunity to grow into a specialist or consulting role

Supportive and collaborative team environment



Start Your Career With Us

If you’re ready to kickstart your engineering career and work on meaningful projects in the energy sector, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now:

Clear thinking

Hook/angle

Offer

Target audience

title: Supply Chain Assistant
description:
INTRODUCTION

Seed & Sprout is a fast-growing Australian direct-to-consumer eCommerce brand creating beautifully designed, planet-friendly products that make sustainable living simple and joyful.

With rapid growth across both DTC and wholesale channels, and increasing operational complexity, the business is investing in strengthening its supply chain foundations to support scale, speed, and accuracy.



ROLE PURPOSE

The Supply Chain Assistant plays a critical operational support role across the end-to-end supply chain.

This role is responsible for ensuring that orders, inventory data, supplier documentation, and logistics processes are accurate, up to date, and flowing smoothly from suppliers ? warehouse ? customers.

The position is highly detail-oriented and process-driven, acting as the backbone of day-to-day supply chain execution.

While the role is administrative in nature, it requires strong ownership, problem-solving, and the ability to work autonomously in a fast-paced environment.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Logistics & Supplier Coordination

Liaise with suppliers to confirm Cargo Ready Dates (CRDs)

Collect, confirm, and file packing lists and commercial invoices

Coordinate with freight forwarders on shipments and delivery timelines

Maintain accurate and up-to-date delivery information in internal systems (GTM, Cin7)

Update shipment timelines and key dates in Cin7 POS

Enter new SKUs into the master SKU sheet

Maintain accurate SKU data including dimensions and weights



FORECAST REPORTING & DATA MANAGEMENT

Update payment dates in line with shipping timelines

Send remittance advice to suppliers

Record and track freight costs and payments

Support broader data gathering for demand planning and reporting



WAREHOUSE COORDINATION

Support daily reporting updates (including demand planning inputs)

Update shipment information for internal stakeholders

Liaise with warehouse teams regarding inbound deliveries

Reconcile received stock against purchase orders in Cin7

Process purchase order receipts accurately in Cin7



CIN7 & SYSTEM MANAGEMENT

Enter and manage purchase orders in Cin7

Process PO receipts and freight-related expenses

Maintain accurate product data (style names, categories, attributes)

Ensure consistency between Cin7 and Shopify data structures

Maintain product dimensions and pallet configurations

Support data cleanliness for reporting and analysis



CURRENT ENVIRONMENT & EXPECTATIONS

Seed & Sprout operates in a high-growth, high-SKU-complexity environment with frequent new product launches and evolving operational processes.

This role requires:

High attention to detail across large volumes of data

Comfort with repetitive but critical tasks

The ability to work independently (approximately 90% autonomous)

Strong communication across suppliers, freight partners, and internal teams



SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

Strong organisational skills and deadline management

Excellent attention to detail and data accuracy

Proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets

Experience with Cin7 (or similar inventory management systems)

Understanding of Shopify and its impact on reporting and operations

Strong relationship management skills (suppliers, freight forwarders, warehouse teams)

Calm, solutions-focused approach under pressure

Ability to work independently and take ownership of tasks



WORKING HOURS & STRUCTURE

Full-time, Monday to Friday (approx. 9am–5pm, with some flexibility)

Fully remote role based in the Philippines

Requires consistent availability aligned with Australian business hours



FIRST 90 DAYS - WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

Develop strong understanding of systems (Cin7, SKU master, payment tracking)

Take ownership of core administrative workflows with minimal supervision

Ensure high accuracy across purchase orders, shipments, and inventory data

Build effective working relationships with suppliers, freight partners, and warehouse teams

Begin supporting automation and process improvement initiatives downstream



WHY JOIN SEED & SPROUT

You’ll be part of a growing, values-driven business with a collaborative remote team, where operational excellence is critical to scaling impact.

This role offers the opportunity to build deep expertise in supply chain operations while contributing to a brand committed to sustainability and thoughtful design.

title: Remote Sales Development Rep
description:
Full-Time Remote Sales Rep

Job Description:

Evolving Document of Agreed Upon Expectations



Full time outbound SDR who finds their own leads, does 100+ outbound touches / day, and closes first orders via our order form, escalating larger accounts to manager and after 90 days of ramp, consistently opens 8–12+ new accounts per month.

Requirements

C1+ English, comfortable on the phone with US business owners.

Stable high?speed internet, headset, quiet workspace.

Prior outbound calling / sales experience.

Comfortable working US CST hours

Expectations

Time Zone: USA CST

You’ll work 8–9 hours/day within the 8am–8pm CST window, 5–6 days/week (50 hrs total).



We measure based on inputs, results, and behaviors. Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) that you need to hit are outlined below. 



Ramp Up Period:

30 Day Expectations – 2-4 new accounts per month

60 Day – 4-6 new accounts per month

90 Days – you should be closing 8-12+ new accounts monthly consistently

Activity Metrics (Inputs)

Hours: Minimum 50 working hours per week.

Primary Actions: 100+ primary actions per day (calls, texts,
s to decision makers at smoke shops). 

Mix: At least 40% of actions = new outreach (not just follow?ups).

200 New Leads added to Pipeline Weekly. New leads = new smoke shops added to CRM with owner contact info. (If a shop has more than one location, it is counted as one account)

Logging in CRM: Every action is logged in the tracker (no log = didn’t happen), including time, type, and outcome. 

Primary actions = any meaningful outbound touch that can move a deal forward.

Consequences: This is the scorecard. If you hit these numbers and live these behaviors, we’re good. If not, we’ll have one correction conversation. If it still doesn’t change, I’ll let you go. That’s not personal; that’s the agreement.

Results Metrics (Outputs)

Weekly

Inputs: 600 Primary Actions

Pipeline:

80–100 smoke shops reached (who at least know your name/company name).

40–60 engaged shops (they replied, took a call, or requested info).

15–20 in “hot” status (clear intent to order / waiting on something small).

New Accounts:

1–2 new accounts closed

Clear path to 2-4+ new accounts in next 2-4 weeks from the existing pipeline.

Behavior Standards

Communicate

Reply to your manager within 20min on all work messages.

Be on time for all scheduled check?ins / roleplays.

If you’re going to miss a metric or a day, you tell me before, not after.

Be Accountable

Accurately log all leads and actions same day.

Own mistakes and bring problems with a proposed solution.

Falsifying activity or results is immediate termination.

Be Coachable

Attend weekly check-ins, roleplay, or training.

Implement feedback on scripts and process quickly.

Protect the Brand

Be respectful and professional with every shop.

Follow all compliance / legal guidelines for outreach.



Consequences: One major breach of trust (lying about activity, disrespecting customers, ghosting) is grounds for immediate termination.



Weekly Reports:

Link is on CRM - Filled out by 5 PM CST every Sunday. 



Note: These are the rules of the game. If you hit the actions and live the behaviors, I’ll back you and invest in you. If you don’t, we agreed up front that I’ll end the role quickly.





Compensation

Base salary: $400 USD per month.

Commission: 10% of the first sale of any New Account you close in a given month (excluding shipping and other pass-through operational costs).

Base salary is contingent on meeting activity requirements. If you consistently miss activity targets, we will end the role.

Commissions are paid on all qualifying New Accounts you close while employed.

New Account = a business that has never ordered from us before (new legal entity).



Next Steps – Application

Step 1 – Written Task (REQUIRED)



Please answer ALL of the questions below in writing and paste your answers into your application:



Confirm Requirements

Are you able to work US CST hours (night shift in your country)?

Do you have C1 or better English and prior outbound calling experience?

What city/country are you in and what is your internet speed (upload/download)?



Cold Outreach Sample (5–8 sentences)

Imagine you are calling a smoke shop owner for the first time. Write what you would say from “Hello” until you ask them to place a small first test order.

Include:

A quick intro (who you are, company name)

Why you’re calling

One clear benefit for ordering from us

A simple call?to?action (next step)



Past Volume & Results

Briefly describe your most recent outbound role:

What was your daily target for calls / messages?

What was your best month (results you achieved)?



Handling the Hard Days (3–5 sentences)

On days you don’t feel like making calls but still must hit 100+ actions, what do you do to push through and hit your numbers?



Step 2 – Voice Test (2–3 minutes total)



Please record a 2–3 minute audio or video (Loom, Vocaroo, unlisted YouTube, etc.) and send the link in your reply. One take is fine. Use English only.



Part 1 (about 60 seconds): Introduce Yourself



Your name, location, and past outbound sales experience.

The most dials / messages you’ve done in a single day.

Why you want a high?volume cold calling role working US CST hours.



Part 2 (about 60–90 seconds): Mock Call



Pretend you’re calling “Joe’s Smoke Shop” for the first time.

Do a real opening like it’s a live call (no reading monotone).

Goal: get Joe interested in placing a small test order or speaking to your manager.

Handle this objection once: “We already have a distributor.”

Record in a quiet place with a stable connection so I can clearly hear your voice, pace, and energy.

title: IT & Systems Administrator
description:
Apply through the below link: 



About the Company

We are a California-based company operating in the environmental health and safety (EHS) industry. With over 35 years in business, we are a stable, established organization that values consistency, clear processes, and the people who keep things running well behind the scenes.





Schedule Note

This role follows California business hours (PST/PDT), which means your shift will be approximately 1:00 AM – 10:00 AM Philippine time (shifting to 2:00 AM – 11:00 AM during daylight saving). Weekends are always off. The schedule is fixed and predictable — no rotating shifts, no surprise overtime.

We're upfront about this because it's a real consideration. If you've worked a graveyard shift before and found it suits your lifestyle, this role offers the stability and long-term security that makes it worthwhile.





About the Role

We're looking for a careful, methodical tech generalist to own our digital operations day-to-day. This is a stable, long-term role with a clear scope — not a chaotic environment where priorities change hourly.

You'll manage the platforms our team relies on, build automations that actually last, document everything you learn, and troubleshoot issues with patience and precision. Most work is independent; you'll have a senior tech lead to collaborate with on bigger initiatives but won't need constant supervision.





What You'll Own

•    Systems & Platforms — Become the resident expert on our core tools: Microsoft 365 (Teams, SharePoint, Power Automate), WordPress, HubSpot, ActiveCampaign, FileMaker, and Dialpad. Handle configurations, updates, and improvements with attention to detail.

•    Automation — Identify repetitive processes and build reliable, well-documented automations that the team will actually use long-term.

•    Documentation — Write SOPs, troubleshooting guides, and process docs. Record training videos. Build the knowledge base that keeps operations consistent.

•    Tech Support & Troubleshooting — Diagnose real problems at the root level — not just surface symptoms — and guide teammates to lasting solutions via chat, call, or screen share.

•    Infrastructure & Systems Management — Maintain and monitor the underlying systems, networks, and environments that keep operations running reliably.

•    Security & Access — Manage user accounts, permissions, and passwords. Keep systems organized before issues arise.







What We're Looking For

•    You learn tools deeply — real expertise, not surface familiarity

•    Documentation comes naturally to you; it's how you ensure quality

•    You work well independently with a defined scope and clear direction

•    You're methodical: you'd rather fully understand a problem than rush a fix

•    You troubleshoot systematically — you trace problems to their root cause, not just the surface symptom

•    You have hands-on experience with tech infrastructure — networks, systems, user environments, or cloud-based setups

•    Stable, predictable work — even at odd hours — suits you better than daytime chaos



Why This Role Stands Out

•    Fixed schedule — same hours every day, no rotating shifts

•    Weekends always off — no exceptions

•    Long-term stability — permanent role, not project-based

•    160 hours/month — full-time, consistent workload

•    Deep ownership — you'll go deep on our systems, not bounce between tasks

•    Calm environment — established team, defined processes, no daily fire drills







Benefits

•    Paid leaves — we provide paid time off so you can rest and recharge without worrying about lost income

•    Paid California public holidays — you will be paid on all California public holidays

•    Stable, long-term engagement — this is not a project-based or contractual role; we're looking for someone to grow with us







Minimum Requirements

•    Bachelor's degree in any field (4-year course), preferably IT, Computer Science, or a related discipline

•    At least 2 years of experience in tech support, IT administration, or infrastructure management

•    Proven troubleshooting experience — hardware, software, network, or systems level

•    Solid Microsoft 365 experience (Teams, SharePoint, Power Automate)

•    Ability to work California business hours (graveyard shift, PH time)





Bonus Points

•    Experience with HubSpot, ActiveCampaign, WordPress, FileMaker, or Dialpad

•    Background in network administration, cloud infrastructure, or endpoint management

•    Experience with process documentation or SOP writing

•    Prior experience in a remote, US-based work setup







How to Apply

Submit a cover letter (300–500 words) addressing the following:

1.    Describe a tech infrastructure or systems problem you troubleshot — what was the issue, how did you diagnose it, and what was the outcome

2.    Walk us through a platform or system you managed and maintained — what tools were involved and how did you ensure it ran reliably

3.    Share an example where your documentation or SOPs made a real difference to a team or process

4.    Describe how you have handled security and access management — how do you manage user accounts, permissions, and passwords, and what does your workflow look like when onboarding or offboarding a user

5.    Walk us through how you approach tech infrastructure — how do you monitor, maintain, and flag issues before they become problems

6.    Why this role appeals to you, and confirm whether you are comfortable working California business hours (graveyard shift, PH time)



Attach your resume along with your cover letter. We review both, but we're more interested in how you think and work than in job titles.

title: Cinematographer / Video Editor / Ai
description:
We are looking for someone who is skilled in cinematography, video editing, and AI tools.



This role is for someone who knows how to capture strong visuals, edit polished videos, and use AI to improve workflow and creative output. We want someone who understands camera work, lighting, composition, storytelling, pacing, and modern AI tools that can support video production.



The ideal person can help create content that feels cinematic, professional, and engaging from start to finish.



What we’re looking for

-Strong video editing skills

-Understanding of lighting, composition, camera movement, and shot selection
